speeches.title: Changes to Bank of Canada Governing Council
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada announced today the departure of Deputy Governor Rhys Mendes on April 10, and the retirement of Deputy Governor Sharon Kozicki on July 15.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada, Export Development Canada, RBC and TD successfully complete bond issuance experiment  using distributed ledger technology
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada (BoC), RBC Capital Markets, RBC Investor Services, TD Bank Group (TD), and Export Development Canada (EDC) successfully completed Project Samara, a collaborative initiative to evaluate how tokenization and distributed ledger technology (DLT) can improve bond issuance and settlement in a real-world setting.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada issues revised compliance order to XTM Inc.
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today issued a revised temporary order to XTM Inc. (XTM). The Bank has authorized XTM to recommence retail payment activities under the supervision of the Monitor appointed today by the Ontario Superior Court of Justice under the Companies' Creditors Arrangement Act, and subject to terms and conditions set out in the Bank's revised order.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada issues immediate compliance order to XTM Inc.
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today issued a temporary order to XTM Inc. (XTM) to immediately cease performing any retail payment activities.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada announces finalists for the eleventh annual Governor’s Challenge
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada has announced the five finalist teams in the 2025-26 edition of The Governor’s Challenge, a competition where university students simulate the role of advisor to the Bank’s Governing Council.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada lowers policy rate to 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today reduced its target for the overnight rate by 25 basis points to 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Triennial Central Bank Survey of Foreign Exchange and Derivatives Market Activity in Canada during April 2025
speeches.summary: In April, the Bank of Canada and 51 other central banks and monetary authorities conducted the latest triennial survey of turnover activity in the foreign exchange (FX) and over-the-counter (OTC) derivatives markets in their respective countries.

items.headline: An Unqualified Good Year For Stocks
items.summary: Are we headed back to US underperformance?

items.headline: Fed's Waller Expresses Caution About Lowering Rates
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller says he is cautious about the need to lower interest rates in the near term due to the energy shock triggered by war in Iran during remarks at Auburn University. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: US Natural Gas Extends Gains on Lower Output, Oil Decoupling
items.summary: US natural gas futures ended higher for a third day, erasing earlier losses triggered by falling oil prices as gas producers continue to scale back supply in anticipation of lower weather-related demand. Traders also bought back US gas contracts at a discount after Iran’s foreign minister saying the Strait of Hormuz was “completely open” caused immediate financial outflows from baskets of energy products.

items.headline: Oil Tankers Hauling US Crude Via Panama Approaching 4-Year High
items.summary: US oil cargoes transiting the Panama Canal are close to a four-year high as Asian refiners rush to import American crude in lieu of Mideast supplies strangled by the weeks-long disruption to Strait of Hormuz shipping.

items.headline: Retail Traders Make Korea Top Place for Risky Exchange Products
items.summary: South Korean investors’ appetite for risk has turned the Korea Exchange into the world’s busiest hub for risky leveraged vehicles.

items.headline: Dollar Shortage Still Looms After Argentina Buys Time at IMF
items.summary: Argentina bought itself time at the International Monetary Fund this week, but President Javier Milei still faces a hard-currency shortage that he’ll have to tackle before elections next year.

items.headline: Bitcoin Climbs to Two-Month High Amid Middle East Deal Optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin rose to the highest level since early February after a flurry of comments from the US and Iran sparked optimism that the conflict in the Middle East may be heading toward a resolution.

items.headline: Hormuz Strait Progress Likely to Reverse Without Breakthrough Says Bob McNally
items.summary: Bob McNally, president and founder of Rapidan Energy Group, said that despite oil market optimism, he believes the Strait of Hormuz will close once again unless the US and Iran make major progress on a deal over the weekend. McNally said that his estimate is that it will take at least 3 to 4 months for the oil market and supply traffic to revert back to pre-war levels once there is a deal and that there are some oil fields that may be permanently closed.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Wall Street Extends April Surge on Peace Prospects: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Speculation the Iran war is poised to end sent risky assets surging anew, extending a month-long rally that’s lifted the S&P 500 Index to a succession of records, added around $12,000 to the price of Bitcoin and pushed up credit and gold.

items.headline: Gold Jumps to One-Month High as Iran Says Hormuz Completely Open
items.summary: Gold jumped to the highest in almost a month after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz “completely open” to commercial traffic, a major step toward ending the conflict that spurred energy prices to rally and threatened economic growth.

items.headline: Short Squeeze Turbocharges Equity Rally as Riskiest Stocks Soar
items.summary: Investors could point to any number of reasons behind the stock market’s neck-breaking rally. A rapid unwind in bearish wagers gone wrong should be near the top of the list.

items.headline: Oil Tankers Start to Dash for Hormuz as Shipowners Seek Answers
items.summary: A handful of oil tankers were racing toward the Strait of Hormuz on Friday after Iran declared it open, even as industry groups urged caution and several shipowners and traders said they needed more clarity about the status of the waterway before making any decisions.

items.headline: Ethiopia Bondholders Start Process to Sue Government Over Defaulted Debt
items.summary: A group of Ethiopia’s bondholders formally began a process to sue the government over a defaulted $1 billion debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.headline: Many Potential 'Spoilers' Remain in Complex US, Iran Deal, Says Natasha Hall
items.summary: Natasha Hall, associate fellow at Chatham House, said that despite positive reaction to the announcements from the US and Iran, the ceasefire is very fragile and there are many complex details still to be worked out.  Hall said that there are potential 'spoilers' that could emerge in negotiations as Iran considers nuclear restrictions and its newfound economic power via the Strait of Hormuz.   (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: SNB’s Schlegel Stresses Higher Willingness on FX Interventions
items.summary: The Swiss National Bank is more willing than usual to step into markets to ensure price stability amid higher energy prices, according to President Martin Schlegel.

items.headline: Air Canada Suspends Summer Flights to JFK Over Rising Fuel Costs
items.summary: Air Canada will suspend daily flights to John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York City amid soaring jet fuel costs.

items.headline: Aluminum Drops After Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is Fully Open
items.summary: Aluminum prices fell after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was open for cargoes to transit, a move that would allow the metal to be exported out of key producers Bahrain, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

items.headline: Brazil Treasury Sees Room for More Foreign-Exchange-Linked Debt
items.summary: Brazil’s government sees room to grow its share of foreign-exchange-linked debt even further following this year’s planned expansion, Treasury Secretary Daniel Leal said in an interview.

items.headline: S&P 500 Heads Toward Best Month Since 2020 on Hope for War’s End
items.summary: Stocks surged Friday, extending a record-setting run that’s sending the S&P 500 Index toward its biggest monthly gain since 2020, as Iran reopened the vital Strait of Hormuz and President Donald Trump said a deal to end the war could be finalized soon.

items.headline: Obesity Firm Kailera Therapeutics Up 63% After $625 Million IPO
items.summary: Kailera Therapeutics Inc. shares surged 63% after the clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on obesity raised $625 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

items.headline: Oil Tankers Make Dash Toward Hormuz as Iran Says Strait Is Open
items.summary: At least eight oil tankers raced toward the Strait of Hormuz in the hours after Iran’s foreign minister said the vital waterway was fully open to shipping.

items.headline: Oil and Gas Plunge on Hormuz Opening, Hope for End of War
items.summary: Oil and European natural gas prices tumbled after the promised re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz bolstered sweeping optimism that the US-Iran conflict will reach an end and ease disruptions to global energy markets.

items.headline: Short Seller Targets AQR Backer Over Tax-Loss Harvesting
items.summary: A short seller is taking aim at the more than $1 trillion that’s invested in strategies that reduce taxes for the rich — putting one of the US’ most staid money managers in its crosshairs.

items.headline: Hedge Funds’ Treasury Bets Risk Market Shock, Apollo’s Slok Warns
items.summary: A buildup of leveraged hedge fund bets in Treasuries has left investors exposed to abrupt position shifts that could amplify stress across global bond markets, according to Apollo Global Management Chief Economist Torsten Slok.

items.headline: Stock Rally, Oil Drops on Hormuz Hope | Open Interest 4/17/2026
items.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Oil slides again as markets react to signals the Strait of Hormuz is open. Netflix disappoints investors as Reed Hastings steps down after nearly three decades. And Anthropic’s CEO heads to the White House as AI tensions cool. Oklo CEO Jake DeWitte explains why nuclear power could fuel the future of space. Brian Kelly reveals why airfare keeps climbing as perks disappear. And Seana Smith shows where investors are placing bets as AI and defense dominate. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: IMF Members Say Iran Conflict Poses Risk to Global Economy
items.summary: The conflict in the Middle East is a major shock that poses a “serious threat to the global economy” and will hit the poorest countries the hardest, according to a statement by the Chair of the International Monetary and Financial Committee.

items.headline: Trump Says Iran Agrees to Unlimited Suspension of Nuclear Program
items.summary: President Donald Trump said in a phone interview on Friday that Iran agreed to suspend its nuclear program indefinitely and will not receive any frozen funds from the US in exchange for the move. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Trump Says Iran Is Removing Mines From Hormuz Strait
items.summary: President Donald Trump says Iran is removing sea mines from the Strait of Hormuz. He also says NATO is now offering to help secure the waterway. Bloomberg's Alaric Nightingale reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Daly Says Fed Policy Is in a 'Very Good Place' Right Now
items.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly says leaving rates unchanged would still restrain inflation and both long- and medium-term inflation expectations are well-anchored during remarks at UC Berkeley. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Brokers Flock to Paradise of Sun, Sand and ‘Unlimited’ Leverage
items.summary: Offshore havens like the Seychelles are enabling online trading firms to offer high-risk bets to retail investors.

events.event_name: U.S. dollar seen strengthening as U.S.-Israel strikes intensify
OPEC+ agrees modest oil output boost even as US war on Iran disrupts shipments
